ops.text: Codex (gpt-5.6-sol) hit its OpenAI usage limit mid-dispatch during the cc-guides redesign review lanes — "You've hit your usage limit... try again at Jul 27th, 2026" — so both finder and refuter codex-wrapper lanes silently degraded to the wrapper's own Claude analysis instead of a second-tool opinion. Every Models-table lane routed through codex (sol implementation/review, luna recon/rote) is unavailable until the quota resets on 2026-07-27; work in this window re-routes to opus/sonnet with fable escalation, and any review that specifically wanted cross-model diversity should be re-dispatched after reset. The wrapper handled it gracefully (explicit blocker disclosure up front), but there's no upfront quota probe — a `codex-ask --ps`-style preflight would have saved two dispatch round-trips.
